steps.n: These are special letters that begin some chapters of the Quran. We know how to say them but their full meaning is known only to Allah.

steps.m: Ha Meem (special letters at start of chapter)
steps.n: The letters are connected and a special mark (ٓ) is added to show these are Quranic opening letters
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
This form shows that something is being done gradually or repeatedly

steps.m: a sending down (subject of this verse)
steps.n: The double damma shows this word is the subject of the verse, meaning 'This is a sending down...'
This is the basic form of the word that means 'from'. It connects two words to show that something comes from somewhere or belongs to something.
The shadda (ّ) doubles the م sound to add emphasis, like saying 'directly from' or 'truly from'
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to the next word الرَّحْمٰنِ (the Most Merciful), telling us something is from the Most Merciful
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ان makes it mean 'full of mercy' or 'intensely merciful'

steps.m: The One who shows complete and endless mercy
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to Allah specifically, as He is The Most Merciful

steps.m: The One who shows complete and endless mercy (connected to مِّنَ before it)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word is connected to the word مِّنَ (from) that comes before it in the ayah
Adding vowels to make it a complete word meaning 'to show mercy'
Changed from a past-tense verb (he showed mercy) to a special form that shows someone who is always very merciful
Added ال (The) at the start to make it a title/name of Allah

steps.m: The intensely and constantly merciful (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to or possessed by something else in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'wrote' to noun meaning 'something written'
The tanwin (double damma) shows this is the subject of the next verb فُصِّلَتْ (were detailed/explained). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.

steps.m: to separate or explain in detail

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show this happened to something. Added shadda for emphasis.

steps.m: it was detailed/explained (referring to ءَايٰتُهُۥ - His verses)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show this refers to His verses (ءَايٰتُهُۥ) which is feminine plural

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة changes this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Changes from singular to plural by adding ات at the end - a common way to make feminine words plural
Adds tanween dammah (double damma) to show this is an indefinite noun
The alif (ا) becomes a long alif (ٰ) and the word is marked as the subject by the -u ending
Adds the possessive pronoun 'his' to show these are Allah's signs/verses that were detailed
Changes the verb into a noun that represents what is recited. The ان ending makes it stronger, showing it's not just any recitation but an important one.

steps.m: as a recitation (describing how the verses were detailed/explained)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing how something was done. Here it explains how the verses (ءَايٰتُهُۥ) were detailed (فُصِّلَتْ) - they were detailed as a recitation.

steps.m: to be clear/express clearly in Arabic

steps.n: Root letters were connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the word for 'Arabs'
Adds ي with shadda to show something belongs to or relates to Arabs
Adds tanween fatha to show this word is describing the Quran mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to stand firmly, to rise up
people, community (who are addressed by قُرْءَانًا)
steps.n: Changes from the action of standing to mean a group that stands together as a community. This word is connected to قُرْءَانًا (Quran) in meaning, showing who the Quran is for.
Adds لِّ (with emphasis) at the beginning to show this is specifically for these people. The shadda (ّ) adds extra emphasis.
Adds tanween (ٍ) to show this refers to any people who have the quality of knowing (يَعْلَمُونَ). The word is in the genitive case because it comes after لِّ.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past

steps.m: they know (referring to a group who should understand)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to people who can understand the Quran's Arabic verses.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (gave news) to a noun meaning someone who gives news

steps.m: a bearer of good news (object of أَرْسَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being sent in the phrase 'We sent a bearer of good news'

steps.m: to warn of danger or consequences
warner (connected to بَشِيرًا - bearer of good news)
steps.n: Changes the verb 'to warn' into 'one who warns' by adding ي between the last two root letters. This word pairs with بَشِيرًا (bearer of good news) to show two aspects of the same role.
The ًا ending shows this is being acted upon in the same way as بَشِيرًا before it
Adds 'and' to connect this word to بَشِيرًا (bearer of good news), showing these are two roles working together
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes the meaning from just 'turning' to specifically 'turning away' or 'avoiding'. Like how turning your back is different from just turning.

steps.m: then he turned away (subject of أَكْثَرُهُمْ)
steps.n: The prefix فَ adds the meaning of 'then/so' to sequence or connect events
The أ at the start makes this a comparative/superlative form meaning 'more' or 'most'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them'. This word is the subject of فَهُمْ (then they) in this ayah.
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group)

The فَ at the start means 'so' or 'then', connecting this to the previous statement. This pronoun هُمْ (they) refers to the same group as أَكْثَرُهُمْ (most of them) and is the subject of يَسْمَعُونَ (they listen/hear).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسْمَعُونَ (they listen/hear) negative, changing its meaning to 'they do not listen/hear'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adds masculine plural ending to show multiple people are doing the action. This word comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not hear'
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense pattern. This is a common change with middle-weak verbs.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (for a group of people)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to form the singular noun 'heart'
Changes from 'heart' to 'hearts' by changing the middle vowel to 'u' and adding 'u' after it

steps.m: our hearts (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: The word 'hearts' becomes 'our hearts' by adding نا (meaning 'our') at the end. This shows who the hearts belong to - they belong to us!
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show this is a preposition that means something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the أَكِنَّة (coverings) are inside their hearts.
Changed the ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This is just a writing style variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to hide or protect something by covering it

steps.n: The root letters were combined with a kasra vowel to make a noun meaning 'a cover' or 'shelter'
Added the feminine marker ة to make it refer to a feminine covering
Added أ at start to make it plural - like how كتاب becomes أكتب (books)
Combined the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together

steps.m: from what (that تَدْعُونَآ you call us to)
steps.n: The ن changes into م and gets a shadda because it's easier to say when the next letter is also م. This is like how 'in memory' sounds like 'im memory' when said quickly in English.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The و returns because it's not at the end anymore.
Added نَآ at the end to show 'us' as the object being called. The آ shows emphasis.
Added proper vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Changed final alif to yaa with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show this means 'to him'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added the elongation mark (ٓ) which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Changes from verb 'to permit' to noun meaning 'ear' by adding damma vowels
Changes singular 'ear' to plural 'ears'
Adds 'our' to show possession
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of being blocked
Added double damma to make it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. Added kasra to show it's a complete word.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added a small م (called 'small meem') after ن to show a special pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun meaning 'between'
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by نَا (us)
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'between us'. This whole phrase is possessed by حِجَابٌ (barrier) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be separate/distinct from something else

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added ِكَ at the end to show 'you' and that this word is possessed by حِجَابٌ (barrier) in the phrase 'between us and between you is a barrier'
Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (blocked) to a noun meaning a physical barrier or screen that blocks
The double-damma at the end shows this is any barrier (not a specific one) and that it's the subject of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he worked' to 'work!' by adding i- at the start and making the word shorter - a simple way to make commands in Arabic

steps.m: so work! (in response to their rejection)
steps.n: Added فَ (so/then) to connect this command with the previous statement about their rejection. The alif changes to a different form (ٱ) after فَ.
This is a basic emphatic word that adds certainty to statements. The shadda shows the ن is doubled.
Added نَا at the end to show this refers to 'we'. This makes it mean 'indeed we' instead of just 'indeed'.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Added ون to make it plural. The word is the predicate of إِنَّنَا (indeed we) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Changed to command form. The middle letter و is dropped because it's a 'weak' letter. This word starts the ayah where Allah tells Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to tell the disbelievers that he is just a human like them.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction (only/just)
The alif is lengthened to show the end of the word, but this doesn't change the meaning
This is the basic form of the first person singular pronoun meaning 'I'
Adding alif at the end completes the standard written form of the pronoun
The small circle (۠) is added above the final alif to distinguish this pronoun from other similar words
Added fathas to connect the root letters together, making a noun that refers to a human being. This pattern is used for basic nouns.
The tanwin (double damma) at the end shows this is indefinite (any human being, not a specific one). This word is the predicate (khabar) telling us what 'I' (أَنَا) am - a human being.

steps.m: to be similar or equal to

steps.n: Root letters م-ث-ل became the noun مِثْل meaning 'like' or 'similar to'
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case
The م is doubled (has a shaddah) to emphasize the word 'like/similar', making it stronger

steps.m: like you all (describing أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ - I am a human)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being compared to. This word describes how the speaker (Prophet Muhammad) is similar to the people he's speaking to.

steps.m: to reveal or inspire divine knowledge

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and changing final ي to ى. This shows something is being revealed rather than someone revealing it.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to help with pronunciation
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Added ي (ya) at the end to mean 'to me', showing I am receiving something. Like adding 'me' to 'to' in English. Connected to يُوحَىٰٓ to show I am receiving the revelation.
This is an emphatic particle that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it
Adding ما makes the emphasis even stronger and connects it to what follows. This word introduces what is being revealed (يُوحَىٰٓ) to the Prophet.
The alif is elongated at the end for added emphasis when reciting

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading
Added a damma (u-sound) at the end to show this word is doing the action
Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Changed the first letter from أ to إِ and connected the letters to make a noun meaning the one who is worshipped

steps.m: a god (one of many possible gods)
steps.n: Adding tanwin (double damma) makes it indefinite, showing this refers to any god rather than a specific one
Added short vowels (fatha and kasra) to connect the root letters and make it mean 'alone'
Added alif after first letter to show this is something that has this quality permanently
Added double damma mark to show this is any 'one', not a specific one
The alif after و was replaced with a small alif (ٰ) above - the meaning stays the same

steps.m: to stand straight, to be upright

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel aa
Added است at beginning to show seeking or trying to do the action

steps.m: so he sought to be straight/upright
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect with previous statement
Changed from past tense 'he was straight' to a command meaning 'you be straight!' by changing the ending vowels
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show this means 'to him'
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he forgave'
Added است at beginning to show seeking/requesting forgiveness
Added وا to make it plural command

steps.m: and all of you seek! (plural command)
steps.n: Added و to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and all of you seek His forgiveness! (plural command)
steps.n: Added هُ to show whose forgiveness to seek
This word expresses a warning or threat of destruction. It's often used to warn of serious consequences.
Added tanween to show this is an indefinite noun
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: to join or associate something with something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'someone who joins partners with Allah' (doer of the action)

steps.m: those who join partners with Allah
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural (more than two)

steps.m: for those who join partners with Allah
steps.n: Added لِ with لْ to show 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for those who join partners with Allah
steps.n: The ل merged with another ل before it, shown by the shadda mark
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال with shadda makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ن at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word is describing the people who don't give zakah.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يُؤْتُونَ (they give), making it mean 'they do not give'.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters together
Added يُ prefix to make it present tense. The hamza moved after the prefix and got a sukun.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. Changed kasra to damma before the plural ending.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word into a noun meaning the act or result of purifying. The ة at the end makes it a specific instance.
Added the definite article ال and doubled the ز with shadda because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُؤْتُونَ (they give)
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' (referring to those who don't give zakah in this ayah)
Adds 'and' to connect this phrase with what came before it
Root letters got vowels (fatha on first, kasra on second) and long alif added after first letter to make word meaning 'last'
Added ة at the end to make the word feminine, specifically referring to the afterlife
Added preposition بِ meaning 'in' and definite article ال
Added kasra at the end because the word follows the preposition بِ
This is a pronoun that means 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the subject that كٰفِرُونَ (disbelievers) is describing.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action (like changing 'teach' to 'teacher')

steps.m: those who reject faith (predicate of هم)
steps.n: Makes it plural to show multiple people. This word describes what 'they' (هم) are in the sentence.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding an exclamation mark in English
Added the kasra under إ and shadda on ن to show this word strongly connects to and affects the next word (ٱلَّذِينَ) in the sentence
This word helps connect descriptions to people, like saying 'the ones who' or 'those who'
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word describes the people who believed (ءَامَنُوا۟) in this ayah.
Added long alif to strengthen the meaning and show active participation in believing
Added plural ending وا to show multiple people believing. This word is connected to لَهُمْ (for them) in the ayah.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: they worked/did (referring to those who believed)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action together. This refers back to 'those who believed' (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: and they worked/did (connecting their belief with their actions)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with their belief mentioned before (ءَامَنُوا). This shows that true faith includes both believing AND doing good actions.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he became good'. The root ص-ل-ح means 'goodness/rightness' and adding the vowels makes it into 'he became good'
Changes from 'he became good' to 'one who is good' by adding ا after first letter
Makes the word feminine plural by adding ات
Makes the word definite by adding ال
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ to show who the action is for. This word connects to أَجْرٌ (reward) in the ayah, showing they have this reward.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to change from action to thing. This pattern is used to make nouns from actions.
The double damma (ٌ) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is the subject telling us what they have. This connects to لَهُمْ which means 'for them'.
Basic noun form with fatha on first root letter, sukun on second root letter, and damma at the end. This word means 'other than' and describes أَجْرٌ (reward) in this ayah.

steps.m: to give favors/gifts or to cut off

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive participle form to show who receives the action

steps.m: cut off/ended (describing a reward that is unending)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this describes أَجْرٌ (reward) in the ayah, meaning the reward is unending
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command. The middle letter و gets dropped when making this command form.
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ that adds certainty to what follows
Adding أَ at the beginning turns the statement into a question. The إِ changes to ئِ when combined with أَ

steps.m: is it indeed you all? (questioning the plural audience)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ at the end shows this question is directed at multiple people

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he rejected' in the past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding damma vowels and making first letter silent (sukun)
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at end to show multiple people are doing the action
Added لَ at beginning to emphasize that this action is definitely happening
This is the base relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Added ال with shadda to make it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'

steps.m: in/by/with the one who (connects to خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show connection to the verb خَلَقَ (created) that comes after it. This بِ shows we're talking about the means or way something was done.

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing
he created (subject of creating the earth in يَوْمَيْنِ)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word tells us who created the earth over two days (يَوْمَيْنِ) mentioned later in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This word is what is being created by خَلَقَ (He created) in this ayah.
Added ٱلْ at beginning to make it specific and َ at end to show it's the object being created
This is a basic preposition that connects the verb 'created' (خَلَقَ) with the time period 'two days' (يَوْمَيْنِ). It shows when the creation happened.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks. The و becomes silent with sukun because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: two days (time period in which اللَّهُ created the earth)
steps.n: Added the dual ending يْنِ to show exactly two of something. This word tells us how long it took Allah to create the earth.

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with ت prefix for 'you' and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ون to show that multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: and you all make/set (as rivals to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous statement about disbelieving in Allah. The full meaning shows they are setting up rivals to Allah.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the action is for
Added optional pronunciation marks (ۥٓ) that don't change the meaning
Forms the basic singular noun for one rival or equal
Changes from single rival to many rivals (broken plural)
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تَجْعَلُونَ (you make/set up)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance
Added لِ to strengthen the pointing meaning
Added كَ to complete the demonstrative meaning. In this ayah, it's pointing to 'Lord of the worlds' that comes right after it.
Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete lordship/mastery
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Changed from verb 'to know' into noun meaning 'sign/mark' by adding vowels
Added alif to make noun meaning 'world' - common pattern for nouns
Added ين at the end to make it mean 'many worlds' instead of just one world
Added ال to make it definite. This word is possessed by رَبُّ (Lord) in رَبُّ ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ (Lord of the worlds).

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put/place something somewhere

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This is the main verb of this part of the ayah, with Allah being the understood subject (He).

steps.m: and He made/placed (connected to previous actions about Earth's creation)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with Allah's previous actions mentioned in the ayah about creating the Earth. This shows the sequence of creation events.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it's used to show location within the earth.
Added هَا at the end to refer back to the earth (الأَرْض) which was mentioned earlier. The هَا is used because earth (الأَرْض) is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to be firmly fixed in place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show who or what does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra before last letter
Added و after first letter to make it plural, showing there are many of these things

steps.m: mountains (because they are firmly fixed in the earth)
steps.n: Same word written two different ways - both mean mountains that are firmly fixed in place
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin. In this ayah, it shows the mountains are placed 'from above' the earth.
Added kasra vowel mark under م to show how the word is pronounced
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'above'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added هَا to show it refers to the earth (which is feminine in Arabic)
Added alif after first letter to intensify meaning
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: in it/her (referring to الْأَرْضَ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to الْأَرْضَ (the earth) which was mentioned earlier. The earth is feminine in Arabic, so we use the feminine pronoun.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he measured out precisely (the provisions in فِيهَآ أَقْوٰتَهَا)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows Allah did this action thoroughly and perfectly. This connects to the next phrase which mentions the provisions (أَقْوٰتَهَا) that were measured out.

steps.m: and he measured out precisely (the provisions in فِيهَآ أَقْوٰتَهَا)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this action with the previous actions Allah did (placing mountains and blessing the earth)
This is a common preposition indicating location or containment. In this ayah, it shows where Allah placed the mountains.
The هَآ refers back to the earth (which was mentioned earlier). The آ is an extended writing of ا for Quranic style, but pronounced the same.
Changed from verb 'to feed' into noun 'food' by adding the damma mark
Added أَ at the start to make it plural - like changing 'food' to 'foods/provisions'

steps.m: its provisions (referring to the Earth's sustenance)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show these provisions belong to the Earth (mentioned earlier in the verse)
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Not derived from a root. In this ayah it shows the time period.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The أ at the start changes it from 'divide into four' to 'make into four'
Added ة to make it a countable number (like saying 'four' instead of 'fourth')
Added kasra to show this number is describing the days that come after it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding hamza at start, doubling middle letter, and adding alif after it
Added tanwin kasra to show this word belongs to the number 'four' (أَرْبَعَةِ) that comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the state of being equal
Added tanween fatha to make it work as an adverb describing how the action was done. This relates to قَدَّرَ (He determined) in the ayah, showing that Allah determined the provisions equally.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from verb 'asked' to noun meaning 'asker' by adding alif and kasra
Added plural ending to show multiple people asking
Added لِ to show the meaning 'for/to'
This word connects events in sequence, showing that one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ (He turned to), showing what Allah did next.
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show doing something completely/thoroughly
Changed initial اِ to ٱ to help pronunciation since Arabic words can't start with a sukoon
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects with the word السَّمَآءِ (the heaven/sky) to show where Allah directed His attention.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding a long a sound and hamza at the end
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at end because it follows the word إِلَى (to/towards) which requires this ending
This is the feminine pronoun meaning 'it' or 'she'. In this ayah it refers to السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven) which is feminine in Arabic.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb 'to smoke' into the noun 'smoke' by adding damma at start and alif after second letter
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). Added fatha vowels to show past tense.

steps.m: and then he said (connected to ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after Allah turned to the heaven (ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ). The فَ here shows sequence of events.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation

steps.m: to it/her (referring to السَّمَاء - the sky)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to السَّمَاء (the sky) which is feminine in Arabic.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added conjunction at beginning to connect to previous word
Added preposition لِ to show direction or purpose
Added definite article to specify this refers to THE earth

steps.m: and to the earth (object of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added kasra ending because this word receives the action (Allah spoke to the earth)
Changed from basic 'come' to a command by adding ٱ at the start. The middle letter changed to ئ to make it easier to say. Now means 'come!' when talking to one person.
Added ي at the end to change it from commanding any person to specifically commanding a female

steps.m: both of you come! (command to two - addressing the sky and earth)
steps.n: Added ا with fatha to make it a command for two things (dual). In this ayah, Allah is commanding both the sky and the earth.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the state of being willingly obedient

steps.m: willingly (describing how the heavens and earth responded to Allah's command ائتيا)
steps.n: Added tanween to make it describe how something is done. This word pairs with كَرْهًا (unwillingly) to show the two possible ways the heavens and earth could respond to Allah's command to come (ائتيا).

This is a connecting particle that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects طَوْعًا (willingly) with كَرْهًا (unwillingly), showing Allah gave the heavens and earth these two options.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the state of being unwilling

steps.m: unwillingly (describing how the heavens and earth came in response to Allah's command)
steps.n: Added tanween to make it describe how something is done. This word pairs with طَوْعًا (willingly) to show the two possible ways the heavens and earth could respond to Allah's command.

steps.m: he said (in response to لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is said in response to addressing the heaven and earth.

steps.m: she said (in response to لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Adding 'ت' to the end changes the subject from 'he' to 'she'.

steps.m: they both (f) said (referring to السَّمَآء and الْأَرْض)
steps.n: Changed from 'she said' to 'they both (feminine) said' by adding ا at the end, written as آ. Shows the heaven and earth answering together.
Added vowels to make the past tense form
Added نا to show 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, this is the heavens and earth responding to Allah's command by saying 'we came willingly'.
Root letters ط-و-ع were joined together with a fatha and sukoon to make a noun meaning 'willing obedience'
Changed from noun 'obedience' to describe someone who does the action of obeying
Makes it plural to show multiple people
Makes it accusative case
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels and changing last letter to ى
The ف prefix shows this action happened after something else
Added vowel marks and alif to show long vowel sound

steps.m: and then He completed/decided them (feminine plural)
steps.n: هُنَّ shows the action was done to multiple feminine things
Added vowel marks to make it a usable word. This word is describing how many heavens (سَمٰوَاتٍ) there are.
Added fatha at the end to show this number is being used as an adjective describing something feminine (the heavens)
Adds اء to make it a noun meaning sky/heaven
Forms the plural of sky/heaven. This word is being counted by سَبْعَ (seven) in this ayah.
Adds two dots below (tanween) to show it's being counted as seven heavens
This is a basic preposition that shows where or when something happens. Here it shows Allah inspired something 'in' each heaven.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added يْنِ at the end to show we're talking about exactly two days
Basic root meaning of divine revelation or inspiration
Added vowel marks to make it past tense 'he revealed'. The ي changes to ى (alif maqsurah) because it's at the end of a past tense verb.
Changed to Form IV by adding أَ at start and وْ after first root letter. This makes the meaning causative.

steps.m: and he caused to be revealed/inspired
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and', connecting this action with the previous one

steps.m: and he caused to be revealed/inspired
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is just a writing convention for this type of word ending, doesn't change meaning

steps.m: in/within (connects يَوْمَيْنِ to the previous verb فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows when or where something happens. Here it connects 'two days' (يَوْمَيْنِ) to the action 'He completed them' (فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ), showing when the completion happened.
Root meaning of totality or completeness
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (سَمَآءٍ meaning 'heaven/sky')

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: Root meaning of height or elevation
Connected the root letters س-م-و with vowels to make 'he/it rose up' in the past
Changes from 'rising up' (verb) to 'the thing that rises up' (noun) by adding ء, giving us the word for sky
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) for proper spelling
Added two dots below (tanween) to show it's 'a sky' or 'some sky' rather than 'the sky'
Basic root meaning of giving orders or commands
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the command or matter itself

steps.m: its command/matter (referring to each heaven mentioned in فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمٰوَاتٍ)
steps.n: Added ها which shows this command belongs to each heaven mentioned earlier
Root meaning of beauty or decoration
The doubled letter makes the meaning stronger - like really making something beautiful
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: Root meaning of height or elevation
Transforms root into noun meaning sky/heaven by adding اء ending
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb زَيَّنَّا (we adorned). Also changed alif to alif madd for Quranic spelling.

steps.m: to be near or close to something
steps.n: Root meaning of nearness or proximity
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the state of being near
Changed و to ي and added ا at end to make feminine form meaning 'nearest/worldly'

steps.m: the worldly life, this world (as opposed to the hereafter)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. The ل combines with د making it doubled (shown by ّ)
Root meaning related to morning light
Added ا after second root letter to create noun form showing the result of shining/illuminating
Added مِ prefix to create noun showing the tool that produces light
Changes from one lamp to many lamps by rearranging letters in a special pattern
Added بِ at beginning to show these lamps are used as tools (with/by means of), and َ at end because it's being used as an object
Root meaning of protection or preservation
Changed from action (protect) to thing (protection) by adding ِ under first letter and ْ on middle letter

steps.m: a protection (being done to the sky)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this protection is being done to the sky (connected to the verb زَيَّنَّا - we adorned)

steps.m: and a protection (being done to the sky)
steps.n: Added conjunction و (and)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Adding لِ makes it point to something far away
The كَ at the end makes it more specific and connects it to what's being pointed to. Here it's pointing to Allah's determination mentioned in the next word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to a noun showing the action itself. The ي makes it more intense, showing very precise measuring.

steps.m: the precise measuring (of العَزِيزِ - the Mighty)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word belongs to or comes from العَزِيزِ (the Mighty One) in this ayah
Root meaning of might or power
Adding ي in the middle shows this quality is very strong - like saying 'very powerful' instead of just 'powerful'
Added ال to show we're talking about THE Mighty One (Allah), and ِ at the end because it follows another word
Root meaning of knowledge or knowing

steps.m: One who knows everything completely (divine attribute)
steps.n: Forms intensive pattern فَعِيل showing complete/perfect knowledge - when used as Allah's name it indicates His perfect and complete knowledge of everything

steps.m: The All-Knowing (One) (attribute of Allah mentioned in تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start and changed the ending to match تَقْدِيرُ (the decree of) that comes before it
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition - like saying 'if' in English

steps.m: then if (connects this condition to what came before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement, showing what happens next
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made (someone) turn away/he caused to turn away
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: they turned away (subject of فَقُلْ - so then say)
steps.n: Added plural marker to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to فَقُلْ (so then say) which comes after it, showing their turning away led to Allah commanding the Prophet to speak.

steps.m: to speak, tell, or say something

steps.n: Changed the root into a command by adding a 'u' sound (ُ) on first letter and making the middle letter silent (ْ). The middle letter 'و' drops out because it's a weak letter that often disappears in commands.

steps.m: then say! (command to one male) (connected to أَعْرَضُوا - they turned away)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after they turned away. It's like saying 'if they turn away, then say...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at start to make the verb mean 'to cause/make someone warn' - this pattern makes actions more direct or intense
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action, and added sukun before it
Added كُمْ to show who received the warning - 'you all'
Added vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he was struck by thunder' in the past
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to create noun meaning 'one thunderbolt'
Changed alif to alif with line above (صٰ) and added tanween at end to show this is the object being warned about
Added vowels to make it into a noun that shows similarity
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object
Word already has all needed vowels and case endings

steps.m: to strike suddenly with a destructive force

steps.n: Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who/what does the action
Added ة to make it a specific noun referring to the destructive force itself
Changed alif to alif with line above for Quranic spelling

steps.m: of a thunderbolt (possessed by عَادٍ وَثَمُودَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this thunderbolt belongs to or is associated with the tribes of Aad and Thamud mentioned right after
Adding ا makes it a past tense verb
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of مِّثْلَ (like) in the phrase 'like the thunderbolt of 'Aad'
This is a proper noun referring to an ancient Arabian tribe mentioned in the Quran who were known for carving homes into mountains

steps.m: Thamud (as the object being warned about)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence, connecting to the warning mentioned earlier (أَنذَرْتُكُمْ)

steps.m: and Thamud (connecting it to عَادٍ mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this tribe to the previously mentioned tribe of 'Aad, showing they both received similar warnings

steps.m: when (referring to the time when جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلرُّسُلُ - the messengers came to them)
steps.n: This is a time word that points to when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it specifically points to when the messengers came to the people.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. Added fatha vowels to show past tense.
Adding the long alif emphasizes the action of coming
Added ت with sukun to show feminine past tense

steps.m: she came to them (subject of إِذْ)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show the object (them). This word comes after إِذْ (when) in the ayah.
Changed the vowel pattern to show multiple messengers
Added ال (the) at the beginning. The ل in ال combines with ر to make a stronger ر sound (shown by ّ). Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or source. In this ayah, it connects 'between' (بَيْنِ) with 'their hands' (أَيْدِيهِمْ) to mean 'from before them'
Added a small ن (called tanwin) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to separate or be between things

steps.n: Changes from verb root to noun meaning 'between'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by the following word أَيْدِيهِمْ (their hands). Together they form the phrase 'between their hands' which means 'in front of them' in Arabic.
Connected the root letters to form the basic singular word for 'hand'
Forms broken (irregular) plural for 'hands' by adding أ at start and changing internal vowel pattern
Adds هِمْ at the end to show the hands belong to them
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: and from (connects to خَلْفِهِمْ - behind them)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects 'from before them' with 'and from behind them' in the complete verse.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'behind'
Added kasra to connect it to the next word
Added هِمْ to show it means 'behind them'
Starting with the word أَنْ which shows that something is about to happen
Added لَا (not) to make the negative form
The نْ combines with ل to make لّ (with shadda). This is a common change in Arabic when these sounds come together - they merge to make a stronger ل sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added وا with special marks (ٓ and ۟) to show this is addressing multiple people
This is a basic particle that introduces exceptions or contrasts. It's not derived from a root.
Added shadda and alif to complete the particle. In this ayah, it connects to تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (worship) to mean 'worship none except...'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'god'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in this ayah. Also changed alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb

steps.m: they said (referring to the disbelievers responding to the messengers)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word shows what the disbelievers said in response to the messengers mentioned earlier in the verse.

This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios that didn't actually happen. In this ayah, it's used with قَالُوا (they said) to show what the disbelievers hypothetically wished would happen.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
Changed to a hollow verb form by adding long alif between first and second root letters
Forms intensive noun 'lord'
Doubling the ب shows intensity of nurturing/caring role
Added damma (u-sound) at the end to show this word is doing the action (like saying 'The Lord' instead of just 'lord')
Added نَا at the end to show this is our Lord. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb لَأَنزَلَ (would have sent down) in this ayah.
Makes the verb causative 'to send down'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to make the action causative - making something else come down instead of coming down yourself
Added لَ at start to add emphasis - like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
This root gives us words about ownership, control, and power. Angels are called this because they have certain powers given by Allah.
Changed the root 'to rule/have power' into 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a (fatha-fatha)

steps.m: angel (with emphasis on divine messenger role)
steps.n: Added alif after second letter to emphasize the role of being a messenger
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple angels
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Added نا at the end to show this refers to "we" (the speakers in this ayah)
This is the basic relative pronoun meaning 'what'. The alif is written long (آ) for emphasis in writing but doesn't change the meaning.
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning. In this ayah, this word connects to كٰفِرُونَ (disbelievers) to show what they disbelieve in.
Forms passive voice
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he sent' to 'was sent' by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel - this makes it passive (something done to someone)
Added تُم to show this happened to multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added pronoun ه to show what the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to ما (what) from بِمَآ earlier in the verse.
Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is extended slightly

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth
steps.n: Forms active participle 'disbeliever'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Adds tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a' rejecter rather than 'the' rejecter
Adds ون to show we're talking about multiple people (specifically men or mixed groups)
Added ا at the end to create the basic particle that introduces a topic

steps.m: then as for (introduces what happened to عَادٌ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is what happened next in the story. This word introduces what happened to the people of 'Aad mentioned right after it.
Basic root meaning of returning or repeating

steps.m: one who returns often (name of ancient tribe)
steps.n: Changed و to ا and added fatha to form a name. This refers to the ancient tribe of 'Aad mentioned in the Quran.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the subject doing the action in فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ (they were arrogant)
Basic meaning of the root letters shows the concept of being large or significant
Added vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding است at the beginning shows someone trying to make themselves seem greater than they are
Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before
Adding وا shows a group of people did this action
This is a basic preposition that shows where something happens. Here it connects 'they were arrogant' (ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where they were arrogant.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end because it follows the preposition 'fi' (in) in the ayah
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to الحَقِّ (the truth). Together بِغَيْرِ الحَقِّ means 'without the truth' or 'against the truth'.
Added shadda (ّ) to double the middle letter, making it stronger to emphasize the truth/reality
Added ٱلْ (al-) at the beginning to point to a specific truth

steps.m: of the truth (connected to بِغَيْرِ - without)
steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) at the end because it comes after بِغَيْرِ (without), showing what they were without
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is very common in hollow verbs

steps.m: they said (referring to the people of 'Ad)
steps.n: Adding وا shows multiple people (3 or more men) did the action. Here it refers to the people of 'Ad mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: and they said (connecting to their previous action of being arrogant)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this to their previous action of being arrogant (فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the ayah

This is a question word meaning 'who'. In this ayah, it's asking 'who is stronger?' as part of the arrogant claim made by the people of 'Ad. It's connected to the word أَشَدُّ (stronger) that comes right after it.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show basic past tense meaning
Adding أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end makes it mean 'stronger than' or 'more intense than'. In this ayah, it's comparing strength with 'us' (مِنَّا)
This is a preposition meaning 'from' - it doesn't come from a root
Added 'us' by combining with نا. The ن of مِن combines with the ن of نا to make نّ. This word connects to أَشَدُّ (stronger) to say 'stronger than us'.
Added damma on first letter and doubled the middle letter to make it into a noun. The final weak letter ي is dropped.
Added ة at the end to make it a feminine noun

steps.m: a strength/power (as a description of أَشَدُّ)
steps.n: Added ً (tanween) to show this describes 'how much stronger' - like saying 'stronger in strength'
This turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adds 'and' to connect this question with what came before it

steps.m: and did they not? (connects to يَرَوْا۟)
steps.n: Adds لَمْ which makes the question negative. This word connects to يَرَوْا۟ (they see) in the next part of the ayah, asking 'did they not see?'

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The middle and last letters (أ and ي) combine into one long ى because they're soft letters that flow together

steps.m: they see (in أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ - 'Do they not see?')
steps.n: Added وْا۟ to show multiple people doing the action. This word is part of a question phrase 'Do they not see?' in the ayah

steps.m: that/indeed (introduces the fact about اللَّهَ being stronger)
steps.n: Adding shadda (ّ) makes this word stronger. It introduces an important fact or statement. Here it connects to the next word اللَّهَ to show that Allah is the one who is stronger than them.
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The word Allah is unique and refers specifically to the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل and fatha at the end to show this is the object being referred to. The hamza at the start is written as ٱ when connecting to previous words.
Added ال at the beginning to make it definite and doubled the ذ letter to connect it smoothly. This word connects two parts of the sentence by referring back to Allah who created them.

steps.m: to create or bring something into existence from nothing

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is Allah doing the action in the past. This word relates to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: he created them (referring to عَادٌ, the people of 'Ad)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was created - referring back to the people of 'Ad (عَادٌ) mentioned at the start of the ayah
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it's the subject of أَشَدُّ (stronger), showing who is stronger.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled final letter
This pattern فَعِيل is often used for qualities that are very strong or permanent
Forms the comparative meaning 'more X than'. In this ayah, it's comparing strength with مِنَّا (than us)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from them (referring to عَادٌ, the people of 'Ad)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the people of 'Ad (عَادٌ) mentioned earlier.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning strength
Added ة to show one specific instance of strength
Added tanween fatha (ً) to show this word is the object being compared in the phrase 'who is stronger than us in strength'

steps.m: to come after or behind something

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'behind' instead of the action of following
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِۦ to show this refers to 'him/it' (the Quran)
The word 'behind' has 'his' attached to it (shown by هِ at the end)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
This form shows that the action happens multiple times or gradually over time
Adding the two damma marks makes the word indefinite (not specific)
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something

The shadda appears because this word comes after a ل in تَنزِيلٌ (revelation). When ن follows ل, the ن sound merges into the ل sound, shown by the shadda. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes someone who has the quality of wisdom
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word belongs to or describes حَمِيدٍ (the Praiseworthy) that comes after it
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to praise'
Added ي between second and third root letters to show something that has this quality strongly
Added tanween kasra to show this describes حَكِيمٍ (Wise) in the phrase 'from One Who is Wise, Praiseworthy'
This is the basic form of the word, which can mean 'what' when used as a question word or relative pronoun.
The shadda (doubling mark) on م turns this word from just meaning 'what' into a word that means 'not' - it makes the sentence negative. Like saying 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha vowels to create past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا following Arabic sound rules.

steps.m: it is said (passive form of يَقُولُ)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at the beginning and ُ at the end. This shows the action is being done rather than someone doing it.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to you (being told in يُقَالُ - it is said)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to 'you'. This word shows who is being spoken to in the phrase يُقَالُ (it is said).
This is a basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. It is not derived from a root.

steps.m: except (what comes after from مَّا يُقَالُ لَكَ - what is said to you)
steps.n: Added marks (shadda and alif) to make the word mean 'except'. It shows that something is different or excluded from what came before it.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يُقَالُ (is said) to form the phrase 'what is said to you'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the past tense verb قِيلَ (was said) that follows it. It makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'indeed it was said' instead of just 'it was said'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added ي to complete the passive voice pattern
Changed the vowels to make it mean multiple messengers instead of just one
Added ال (the) to make it 'the messengers' instead of just 'messengers'. The ر gets a shadda mark when ال is added.
Added لِ to show the messengers are receiving something (what was said)

steps.m: to the messengers (object of قِيلَ - was said)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the word لِ. This word receives the action of قِيلَ (was said) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it connects with قَبْلِكَ (before you) to show the message came from earlier times.
Added vowels to make the root into a noun meaning 'before'
Added ِكَ at the end to show this means 'before you'. This word comes after مِن (from/before) in the ayah.
Basic form of the emphatic particle
Added kasra and shadda to show this is emphasizing what comes after it. In this ayah, it's emphasizing 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: lord (one who intensely nurtures and cares for)
steps.n: The shadda makes this word mean someone who does this action constantly and perfectly, making it a title for someone in authority who takes care of others
Adds كَ to show this is your Lord. This word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which is why it has this form.
Basic word meaning 'possessor' or 'owner of' something. In this ayah, it's used to describe Allah as the possessor of forgiveness.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'. This emphasizes that Allah is definitely the possessor of forgiveness.

steps.m: to forgive, pardon, or cover up faults

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to forgive' into the noun 'forgiveness'

steps.m: a forgiveness (possessed by رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added the two dots under (tanween) to show it's 'a forgiveness' - this connects to لَذُو (surely possessor of) in the ayah
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject form of the word meaning 'possessor/owner of'. This word is special because it always needs something after it to show what is possessed.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'. This word connects to مَغْفِرَةٍ (forgiveness) to mean 'possessor of forgiveness'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to make it a noun meaning the consequence or result of following/pursuing someone, which came to mean punishment
Added the tanween kasra ending because this word comes after ذُو (possessor of), making it genitive case

Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) because this word is describing عِقَابٍ (punishment) in the phrase ذُو عِقَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (owner of painful punishment)

Added sukun on last letter to prepare for suffix
Added نا to make it 'we' (first person plural)

Word already has the required vowel marks

Adding ان makes it mean 'the complete/perfect recitation'

steps.m: a Quran (object of جَعَلْنٰهُ - we made it)

Added يّ to show this describes something's quality

steps.m: it was thoroughly explained (referring to the verses/signs)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت to show it refers to آيات (verses/signs) mentioned in the ayah

Shows this word comes after the word فِى (in) in the ayah
This is the basic preposition في meaning 'in'. The ى with ٓ (alif) is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added vowel marks (fatHa and sukun) to the root letters to make the noun meaning 'self/soul'
This is a broken plural form that changes the internal structure of the word to show multiple selves/souls
Added kasra to prepare for possession suffix
Added هِمْ to show these souls belong to them
This is a basic particle that shows something continues up to a certain point. The shadda makes the ت sound stronger.

steps.m: until (connects to يَتَبَيَّنَ - it becomes clear)
steps.n: The ىٰ (alif maqsura) at the end is part of the particle's standard written form. This particle introduces the result or endpoint of the previous statement - here it connects to 'it becomes clear' (يَتَبَيَّنَ).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter changes the meaning from being clear to making something clear
Adding تَ shows the clarity happens on its own, without someone making it clear

steps.m: it becomes clear (until it becomes clear to them that it is the truth)
steps.n: Adding يَ shows this is happening in the present/future. This word comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) in the ayah, showing the signs will continue until the truth becomes clear.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for/to them (connected to يَتَبَيَّنَ - becomes clear)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them'. This word connects to يَتَبَيَّنَ (becomes clear) in the ayah, showing what becomes clear to them.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause and connects it to what came before. It shows that what follows is certain or emphasized.
Added the pronoun هُ (meaning 'it') to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to الْحَقُّ (the truth).

steps.m: to be true/right, to be real/genuine

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it 'he was true' in the past tense
The doubled middle letter turns the action into a noun representing the essential concept
Adding definite article to make the noun definite

steps.m: the truth/reality (predicate of أَنَّهُ - that it is)
steps.n: The damma ending shows this word completes the meaning of أَنَّهُ (that it is) in the ayah
This is a question particle that turns what follows into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention
Adds 'and' to connect this question with the previous statement

steps.m: and has/did not...? (questioning what follows in يَكْفِ)
steps.n: Adds 'not' (لَمْ) to make the question negative - like changing 'and is it?' to 'and has it not?' Makes the question express surprise about something that hasn't happened.
Added fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and final vowel to kasra

steps.m: he suffices (in response to أَوَلَمْ - is it not sufficient?)
steps.n: Final ي drops because it comes after أَوَلَمْ (is it not...) in a question - this is a special grammar rule when asking 'is it not enough?'
Added shadda to show intensity of meaning, making it a noun for someone who nurtures/cares for others completely
Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to the person being spoken to

steps.m: by your Lord (object of يَكْفِ - is sufficient)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show this is being used as proof or evidence in the sentence. This word is connected to يَكْفِ (is sufficient) in the ayah.
Basic emphatic particle that introduces a clause. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis.
Added هُ (with extension mark ۥ) at the end to show 'he'. This word connects to الْحَقُّ (the truth) in this ayah, showing 'that he is the truth'.
Basic preposition indicating position or authority over something. Often used with verbs to show what something affects or relates to.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a fixed spelling for this word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, meaning 'of every thing'

steps.m: to exist, to be a thing

steps.n: Root letters combine to make the simple noun meaning 'thing' - one of the most common words in Arabic
Added the tanwin kasratayn (ٍ) to show this word is indefinite and is owned by كُلِّ (every/all). The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza.

steps.m: one who witnesses (describes Allah's witnessing over everything in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show who does the action. This word describes Allah's complete witnessing over all things, mentioned in أَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (that He is over all things)

steps.m: All-Witnessing (describes Allah's attribute of witnessing over everything)
steps.n: The added double-dammah (ٌ) at the end makes this a complete word that stands on its own, showing Allah's perfect and complete witnessing over everything
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative word to an attention-grabbing word, like saying 'Hey!' or 'Listen!'

steps.m: Now surely! (draws attention to إِنَّهُۥ that follows)
steps.n: The long alif (آ) at the end shows that we pause here when reading. This word introduces an important statement that comes after it.
Added shadda and kasra to show this is an emphatic particle that strengthens what comes after it
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about (they/them)
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows they are 'in' a state of doubt (مِرْيَةٍ)
Changed from basic root letters to a word meaning 'doubting' by adding vowel marks
Added ة to make it a specific instance of doubt
Added the -in sound at the end to show it means 'in doubt' because it follows the word 'in' (fee)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - connected letters with kasra
The dot under the م disappears and a shadda is added on top because it combines with a similar sound before it

steps.m: to meet or come face to face with someone/something

steps.n: Changed from action to the event itself, like changing 'meet' to 'meeting' in English
Makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'a real meeting' instead of just 'a meeting'

steps.m: of the meeting (with رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Shows this word belongs to or connects with the word after it (their Lord). Like saying 'meeting of' instead of just 'meeting'

steps.m: to care for and help grow
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them pronounceable

steps.m: one who completely cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing

steps.m: one who completely cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Added kasra to connect with the next change

steps.m: their Lord (the one who completely cares for and helps them grow)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this refers to their Lord
Starting with the basic negative particle لا
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative word to an attention-getter, like saying 'Hey!' or 'Listen!' in English
Adding the long alif (آ) at the end makes the word stronger and more emphatic. In this ayah, it introduces an important statement about how those who deny are in doubt about meeting their Lord.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's one of the special words that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added هُ to show who is being talked about. This word connects to مُّحِيطٌۢ (encompassing) in the ayah, telling us who is doing the encompassing.
The doubled ل shows this means 'every single one' without exception
The ِ shows this connects to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it
The word بِكُلِّ (with every) connects to مُّحِيطٌۢ (encompassing) to say 'encompassing with every [thing]'
The root letters combine to make the simple noun meaning 'thing' or 'something'
The ٍ at the end shows this word is indefinite and in the genitive case (after a preposition)
The word already has kasra tanwin - there is no change between the before and after word except for the spelling of hamza. The meaning shows it belongs to كُلِّ (every/all)
Basic verb pattern with vowels added to make 'he surrounded/protected' from the root letters
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels.

steps.m: All-Encompassing (a name of Allah, predicate of إِنَّهُۥ)
steps.n: Added shadda on م because it comes after another م sound. Added tanwin (ٌۢ) to show this describes Allah in the sentence 'Indeed, He is All-Encompassing of all things.'
